per_turn_leaks.student_message: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Sometimes I feel nauseous or have stomach upset in the morning after waking up with diarrhea.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I haven't had any fevers or night sweats that I can think of. But I have noticed a low-grade fever a few times a week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been trying to manage my symptoms on my own and haven't seen a doctor in over 2 years, so I'm not really sure what's going on. But I have been taking ibuprofen for joint pain, which seems to make my abdominal symptoms worse.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. That's been tough for our whole family.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I drink a couple of beers most weeknights, but lately, I've actually increased that to 4-5 per night because my symptoms and work have been stressing me out so much.
